skill.name: widgets-ui
skill.description: Declarative UI widgets for React and Next.js applications. The skill documents how agents can render forms, cards, layouts, inputs, and actions from structured widget data.
skill.summary: Render structured agent responses as reusable React and Next.js UI widgets.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, and documentation text in SKILL.md. The real concern is a user-facing install command that runs npx and fetches a component registry from an external URL, so publication is acceptable only with a supply-chain warning.

medium_findings.title: External Package Install Command
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs users to run npx shadcn@latest add against an external registry URL. This is a legitimate installation pattern for UI components, but it can execute package-manager code and retrieve remote content, so users should review the fetched registry before running it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly shown in a bash Quick Start block and uses npx plus a remote URL. The surrounding context is normal component installation documentation, so the concern is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Documentation And Media Links
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to ui.inference.sh, cloud.inference.sh, inference.sh documentation, and an example image URL. These links support documentation and examples, and no evidence of credential exfiltration or hidden network execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links or sample image fields in documentation. I did not find evidence that the skill silently sends local data to these endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives In Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography findings map to Markdown fences, inline code, table formatting, and descriptive text. No executable Ruby, MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows documentation examples and Markdown syntax, not Ruby shell execution or weak cryptographic code. The file contains no executable source file beyond user-facing snippets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Registry Installation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends installing UI components through npx from an external registry. This pattern requires normal package-manager caution because remote dependencies can change outside the skill package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and reproducible at the cited location. It is potentially risky in general, but the documentation context does not show malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Render Agent UI Widgets from JSON
content.value_statement: Agents often return structured data that still needs a usable interface. This skill shows how to render that data as React and Next.js widgets for forms, cards, layouts, and actions.
actual_capabilities: Documents installation of a widget renderer through the shadcn registry command.
Shows how to render structured widget objects with WidgetRenderer.
Defines supported widget categories for layout, typography, inputs, buttons, images, and dividers.
Provides examples for flight cards, contact forms, gradients, and action handling.
Links to widget schema and overview documentation for deeper implementation details.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include local component source files in this package.
It depends on external inference.sh and ui.inference.sh resources for installation and reference material.
It assumes a React or Next.js project that can use shadcn-style registry components.
It does not validate widget payloads or sanitize rendered content by itself.

use_cases.title: Build dynamic agent forms
use_cases.description: Render forms generated by an AI agent, then handle submit and click actions in a React application.

use_cases.title: Display structured results
use_cases.description: Turn structured travel, support, or product data into readable cards with layout, typography, icons, and badges.

use_cases.title: Prototype generative interfaces
use_cases.description: Test declarative UI patterns where an agent emits widget data instead of fixed page components.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple widget
prompt_templates.prompt: Use widgets-ui to design a simple card with a title, body text, and one action button for a React app.

prompt_templates.title: Convert data into UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use widgets-ui to turn this structured result into a readable card layout with labels, badges, and clear action buttons.

prompt_templates.title: Design an agent form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use widgets-ui to define a contact form with text inputs, a message field, a select field, and submit handling guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a widget renderer integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use widgets-ui to plan how a Next.js app should validate, render, and handle actions from agent-generated widget payloads.

output_examples.input: A support agent needs to collect contact details and a short issue description.
output_examples.output: A contact form layout with name, email, issue category, message fields, and one submit action.

output_examples.input: A travel assistant returns departure, arrival, and status information for a flight.
output_examples.output: A compact flight card with route labels, airport codes, a status badge, and a primary action.

output_examples.input: A product assistant returns three subscription options.
output_examples.output: A three-column comparison layout with plan names, feature summaries, price labels, and selection buttons.

best_practices: Review remote registry content before running the npx installation command.
Validate agent-generated widget payloads before passing them to the renderer.
Keep action handling explicit, and route each action type through application-owned logic.
anti_patterns: Do not render untrusted widget data without validation or content sanitization.
Do not treat generated widget actions as authorization decisions.
Do not run remote install commands in production environments without dependency review.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build React and Next.js interfaces from structured widget data returned by agents or application logic.

faq.question: Does the skill include component source code?
faq.answer: No. The package is a documentation skill that points users to a shadcn registry installation command.

faq.question: Can it render forms?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation includes inputs, textareas, selects, checkboxes, buttons, and form submission examples.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the install command?
faq.answer: Treat it like any remote package install. Review the registry output and dependency changes before running it.

faq.question: Does it work outside React or Next.js?
faq.answer: The documented renderer is intended for React and Next.js projects, especially those using shadcn-style components.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for using the documentation.
